incident_summary.title: A resource of type ECSCluster has executed a suspicious command.

decision_questions: ECS cluster arn:aws:ecs:us-east-1:123456789012:cluster/sample-cluster 및 task arn:aws:ecs:us-east-1:123456789012:task/sample-cluster/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012 에 대한 네트워크 차단 조치가 서비스 중단을 허용할 수 있습니까?
원격 통신 차단이 필요하다면 public IP 198.51.100.0 에 대한 차단을 승인할 수 있습니까?
vpc-generatedvpcid 에 대해 VPC Flow Logs 활성화가 가능한 상태입니까?
escalation_assessment.approval_notes: Level 1은 이미 수행되었습니다. 본 권고는 승인형 Level 2 및 Level 3 후보 조치이며, ECSCluster 리소스이므로 EC2 전용 조치는 제외했습니다.

reasoning_bullets: GuardDuty RuntimeMonitoring finding indicates a suspicious command executed in ECS workload context, so additional containment and monitoring beyond Level 1 is warranted.
Provided regulation chunks emphasize network restriction, segmentation, security monitoring, and audit response after a security event.
Candidate actions were filtered to avoid EC2-only actions because the incident resource type is ECSCluster.
Level 2 focuses on lower-impact monitoring and scoped network blocking; Level 3 adds stronger containment-oriented network restrictions.

regulations.excerpt: Monitor, encrypt and restrict communications between environments to only authenticated and authorized connections, as justified by the business.
regulations.why_relevant: Suspicious command execution in the ECS workload indicates possible unauthorized communications or follow-on activity, making network monitoring and restriction directly relevant.

regulations.excerpt: Design, develop, deploy and configure applications and infrastructures such that CSP and CSC (tenant) user access and intra-tenant access is appropriately segmented and segregated, monitored and restricted from other tenants.
regulations.why_relevant: The finding may indicate lateral movement risk, so stronger segmentation and traffic restriction around the affected ECS environment is relevant.

regulations.excerpt: Identify and monitor security-related events within applications and the underlying infrastructure. Define and implement a system to generate alerts to responsible stakeholders based on such events and corresponding metrics.
regulations.why_relevant: A runtime suspicious-command event requires enhanced monitoring, alerting, and triage to support timely response and stakeholder awareness.

regulations.excerpt: Audit encryption and key management systems, policies, and processes with a frequency that is proportional to the risk exposure of the system with audit occurring preferably continuously but at least annually and after any security event(s).
regulations.why_relevant: After a security event, compliance-oriented audit activity is relevant to determine whether related key-management or encryption controls require review.

regulations.excerpt: Monitor security audit logs to detect activity outside of typical or expected patterns. Establish and follow a defined process to review and take appropriate and timely actions on detected anomalies.
regulations.why_relevant: The suspicious command is an anomalous runtime event, so log analysis, triage, and response tracking are directly applicable.

recommended_actions.description: Lower-impact response for the affected ECS environment focused on network visibility and scoped external blocking while preserving service continuity where possible.

selected_playbook.description: Lower-impact response for the affected ECS environment focused on network visibility and scoped external blocking while preserving service continuity where possible.

alternative_playbooks.description: Higher-impact containment for the affected ECS environment using stronger network restriction and monitoring actions to reduce lateral movement and external communication risk.
alternative_playbooks.why_not_selected: Not selected as primary response; router committed to L2. (High severity (9.0) but no L3 triggers → prefer L2.)

route_reasons: High severity (9.0) but no L3 triggers → prefer L2.
